title: tozzettia agrestis
intro: Tozzettia agrestis is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the woodsorrel or wild strawberry vine due to its resemblance to the wild strawberry plant. The medicinal properties of this plant include its high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Tozzettia agrestis include its potential to act as a natural diuretic, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. To prepare this herb, it is often used in teas, tinctures, or dried and added to salads, although it is essential to consume it in moderation due to its high oxalic acid content.
